get.summary: Returns a list of DataAgents from the specified workspace.
get.description:
This API supports [pagination](/rest/api/fabric/articles/pagination).

## Permissions

 The caller must have a *viewer* workspace role. 

## Required Delegated Scopes

Workspace.Read.All or Workspace.ReadWrite.All

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

parameters.name: continuationToken
parameters.description: A token for retrieving the next page of results.

parameters.name: recursive
parameters.description: Lists items in a folder and its nested folders, or just a folder only. True - All items in the folder and its nested folders are listed, False - Only items in the folder are listed. The default value is true.

parameters.name: rootFolderId
parameters.description: This parameter allows users to filter items based on a specific root folder. If not provided, the workspace is used as the root folder.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* InvalidItemType - Invalid item type.

post.summary: Creates a DataAgent in the specified workspace.
post.description:
This API supports [long running operations (LRO)](/rest/api/fabric/articles/long-running-operation).
To create DataAgent with a public definition, refer to [DataAgent](/rest/api/fabric/articles/item-management/definitions/data-agent-definition) article. 

 ## Permissions 
 The caller must have a *contributor* workspace role. 

## Required Delegated Scopes

 Item.ReadWrite.All 

## Limitations 
- To create a DataAgent the workspace must be on a supported Fabric capacity. For more information see: [Microsoft Fabric license types](/fabric/enterprise/licenses#microsoft-fabric-license-types).

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

202.description: Request accepted, DataAgent provisioning in progress.

Location.description: The URL of the operation status, which can be used to track the operation state.

x-ms-operation-id.description: The operation ID which can be used with long running operations (LRO) APIs to track the operation state and get the result.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* InvalidItemType - Item type is invalid

* ItemDisplayNameAlreadyInUse - Item display name is already used.

* CorruptedPayload - The provided payload is corrupted.

* WorkspaceItemsLimitExceeded - Workspace item limit exceeded.

get.summary: Returns properties of the specified DataAgent.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* ItemNotFound - The requested item was not found.

patch.summary: Updates the properties of the specified DataAgent.
patch.description:
## Permissions

 The caller must have *read and write* permissions for the DataAgent.

## Required Delegated Scopes

 Item.ReadWrite.All

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* ItemNotFound - The requested item was not found. 

* InvalidRequest - Invalid update request.

delete.description:
## Permissions

 The caller must have *write* permissions for the DataAgent. 

## Required Delegated Scopes

 Item.ReadWrite.All

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

parameters.name: hardDelete
parameters.description: Specifies whether to perform a hard delete. When set to `true`, the item is permanently deleted and cannot be recovered. When set to `false` or not specified, the item is soft-deleted if the item type supports it.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* ItemNotFound - The requested item was not found.

* InsufficientWorkspaceRole - User doesn't have sufficient workspace role.

* FeatureNotAvailable - This could be due to the soft deletion feature not being available.

* UnsupportedItemType - This could be due to the soft deletion feature not supported by the requested item type.

* TenantSwitchDisabled - This could be due to the soft deletion feature being disabled by the tenant admin.

* InvalidTargetItemStateForSoftDeletion - The item is in invalid states for soft deletion.

* InvalidParentItemStateForSoftDeletion - The item's parent item is not in Active state.

post.summary: Returns the specified DataAgent public definition.
post.description:
This API supports [long running operations (LRO)](/rest/api/fabric/articles/long-running-operation).

When you get a DataAgent's public definition, the sensitivity label is not a part of the definition.

## Permissions

 The caller must have *read and write* permissions for the DataAgent. 

## Required Delegated Scopes

 Item.ReadWrite.All

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The format of the data agent public definition.

202.description: Request accepted. Retrieving the definition is in progress.

Location.description: The URL of the operation status, which can be used to track the operation state.

x-ms-operation-id.description: The operation ID which can be used with long running operations (LRO) APIs to track the operation state and get the result.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* OperationNotSupportedForItem - Operation not supported for requested item.

post.summary: Overrides the definition for the specified DataAgent.
post.description:
This API supports [long running operations (LRO)](/rest/api/fabric/articles/long-running-operation).

 
Updating the DataAgent's definition, does not affect its sensitivity label. 

## Permissions

 The caller must have *read and write* permissions for the DataAgent. 
## Required Delegated Scopes

 Item.ReadWrite.All

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

parameters.name: updateMetadata
parameters.description: When set to true and the .platform file is provided as part of the definition, the item's metadata is updated using the metadata in the .platform file

202.description: Request accepted. Update definition is in progress.

Location.description: The URL of the operation status, which can be used to track the operation state.

x-ms-operation-id.description: The operation ID which can be used with long running operations (LRO) APIs to track the operation state and get the result.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* OperationNotSupportedForItem - Operation not supported for requested item. 

* CorruptedPayload - The provided payload is corrupted.

delete.summary: Deletes a schema element that is no longer present in the live schema from a staging datasource.
delete.description:
> [!NOTE]
> Data Agent configuration management is currently in Preview ([learn more](/fabric/fundamentals/preview)).

Use this API to remove a stale element from the saved datasource schema after it has been removed from the live datasource schema. This API does not automatically remove elements when the live schema changes, so callers can review the change and decide whether to delete the saved element themselves.

An element can be deleted only when it is no longer present in the live datasource schema. If the element still exists in the live datasource schema, the request fails.

## Permissions

The caller must have *read and write* permissions for the DataAgent.

## Required Delegated Scopes

Item.ReadWrite.All or DataAgent.ReadWrite.All

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* ItemNotFound - The requested item was not found.

* ElementStillAvailable - The element still exists in the datasource schema and cannot be deleted.

> Data Agent configuration management is currently in Preview ([learn more](/fabric/fundamentals/preview)).

## Permissions

The caller must have *read and write* permissions for the DataAgent.

## Required Delegated Scopes

Item.ReadWrite.All or DataAgent.ReadWrite.All

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* ItemNotFound - The requested item was not found.

get.summary: Returns schema elements at a level of the schema tree for a published datasource.
get.description:
> [!NOTE]
> Data Agent configuration management is currently in Preview ([learn more](/fabric/fundamentals/preview)).

Elements represent the schema tree of a datasource (schemas, tables, columns). Navigate level-by-level using the `rootId` parameter.

This API supports [pagination](/rest/api/fabric/articles/pagination).

## Permissions

The caller must have *read* permissions for the DataAgent.

## Required Delegated Scopes

Item.Read.All or Item.ReadWrite.All or DataAgent.Read.All or DataAgent.ReadWrite.All

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

parameters.name: rootId
parameters.description: The identifier of a parent element. Omit to get the root level.

parameters.name: continuationToken
parameters.description: A token for retrieving the next page of results.

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

post.summary: Publishes the staging configuration of the specified DataAgent.
post.description:
> [!NOTE]
> Data Agent configuration management is currently in Preview ([learn more](/fabric/fundamentals/preview)).

Promotes the current staging state to the published environment, making it live.

## Permissions

The caller must have *read and write* permissions for the DataAgent.

## Required Delegated Scopes

Item.ReadWrite.All or DataAgent.ReadWrite.All

## Microsoft Entra supported identities

This API supports the Microsoft [identities](/rest/api/fabric/articles/identity-support) listed in this section.

| Identity | Support |
|-|-|
| User | Yes |
| [Service principal](/entra/identity-platform/app-objects-and-service-principals#service-principal-object) and [Managed identities](/entra/identity/managed-identities-azure-resources/overview) | Yes |

## Interface

429.description: The service rate limit was exceeded. The server returns a `Retry-After` header indicating, in seconds, how long the client must wait before sending additional requests.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

default.description:
Common error codes:

* ItemNotFound - The requested item was not found.

* InvalidRequest - Invalid request.

post.summary: Reverts the staging environment of the specified DataAgent to the published state.
post.description:
> [!NOTE]
> Data Agent configuration management is currently in Preview ([learn more](/fabric/fundamentals/preview)).

Discards all unpublished changes in the staging environment.

## Permissions

The caller must have *read and write* permissions for the DataAgent.
